user_query:
I have a small Apple Health export snapshot I want proactively analyzed for early warning signs. Please read the metrics from input/health_data.json and thresholds from input/config.json and generate four outputs:

1) output/readings.json — Normalize the data into three arrays: "temperature", "heart_rate", and "sleep_hours". Each array must contain objects with keys {"timestamp", "value"} and be sorted in ascending timestamp order. Do not add or remove readings.

2) output/baselines.json — Compute per-metric baselines using ONLY the historical portion (exclude the most recent readings so they don’t skew the baseline):
   - temperature: use all temperature readings except the latest one
   - heart_rate: use all heart_rate readings except the latest 10
   - sleep_hours: use all sleep_hours readings except the latest 3 nights
   For each metric present a JSON object with keys {"mean", "stdev", "min", "max", "count"}. Use sample standard deviation.

3) output/alerts.json — Generate alerts if and only if these conditions are met (use the provided thresholds; do not use system time — determine recency by the order of readings in the file):
   - Temperature high: If the latest temperature value is >= thresholds.temperature_high_f. Severity: "critical" if value >= 101.5, otherwise "warning". Include fields: {"type": "temperature_high", "severity", "value", "threshold", "timestamp", "message"}.
   - Heart rate high: Compute the arithmetic mean of the last 10 heart_rate readings (or fewer if fewer than 10 exist). If that average >= thresholds.heart_rate_high, create an alert with fields: {"type": "heart_rate_high", "severity": "warning", "value" (the 10-reading avg), "threshold", "timestamp" (timestamp of the most recent heart_rate reading), "message"}.
   - Sleep degradation: Compute the mean of the last 3 sleep_hours readings and compare it to the baseline mean computed in step 2 for sleep_hours. If recent_avg < baseline_mean * 0.70, create an alert with fields: {"type": "sleep_degradation", "severity": "info", "value" (the 3-night average), "baseline" (the baseline mean from baselines.json), "timestamp" (the most recent sleep entry), "message"}.

4) output/summary.md — A short, human-readable summary including the latest value per metric and any alerts that were triggered.

Important rules:
- Use the data exactly as-is from input/health_data.json. Assume Fahrenheit units for temperature. Do not use the current date/time in any calculation; determine "latest" strictly by the last element in each metric array.
- Baselines must match the exclusion rules above and use sample standard deviation.
- All outputs must be valid JSON/Markdown and must be written exactly to the specified output/ paths.
